title: [ipv6_addr.h:68-91] struct in6_addr fallback detection misses some platforms
description:
Round 2 gap analysis: Missed because header detection logic is complex.

At lines 68-91, the fallback struct in6_addr detection cascade checks various guards. However:

1. Line 74-76 checks for several BSD/Linux guards but misses some:
   - __NetBSD__ platforms may use different guards
   - Cygwin/MinGW may have different conventions

2. The detection relies on preprocessor guards being defined BEFORE this header is included. If ipv6_addr.h is included first (before netinet/in.h), the fallback definition is used, then including netinet/in.h later causes 'redefinition of struct in6_addr'.

The header comment at lines 82-86 acknowledges this and suggests LICHEN_HAVE_IN6_ADDR override, which is the correct escape hatch.

Not a bug per se - the header is defensive and provides an override. But the detection could be more robust with additional platform checks.
close_reason: Fixed: Added Cygwin/MinGW/NetBSD guards, removed incorrect _SYS_SOCKET_H check
